observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:
1. The MTO to submit the abovementioned disbursement vouchers with its
supporting documents for CY 2024 to the MAO and strictly adhere to the
check disbursement process provided under Section 44 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs;
2. The MAO and the MTO to exert all efforts in locating the missing
disbursement vouchers for CYs 2017 to 2021, otherwise, non-presentation
of such shall result in the issuance of an appropriate audit action; and
3. The Municipal Accountant to draw journal vouchers to record the
reconciling items related to the missing disbursement vouchers, as outlined
in Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011, and submit them to the Audit
Team once they are located.
We also recommended that the LCE, through the MTO and the MAO, strictly
comply with the aforementioned guidelines and take proactive measures to
improve communication and coordination between the two departments in
order to prevent the loss of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents,
as well as ensure accurate recording of all transactions and fair presentation of
the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Review and update the E.O. No. 07 s. 2024 to ensure that the frequency of
inventory counts and the submission of related reports are clearly
established. These procedures should align with the NGAS Manual
requirements, specifically mandating semi-annual physical inventory
counts, with RPCIs submitted to the Auditor by July 31 and January 31 of
each year;
2. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to actively oversee the
inventory process, ensuring that the inventory count and related reports
are certified by the committee. This will ensure accuracy and integrity in
35
the reporting process, making the inventory reports reliable and
transparent;
3. Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of supplies and materials
and require coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the GSO
Designate towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method
in recording of supplies and materials; or if impracticable, ensure the
cooperation of the end-users and the GSO Designate to conduct an
inventory of supplies and materials issued to their offices at year-end and
forward the schedule of inventory on hand to the Municipal Accountant for
adjustment of the inventory balances and expense accounts; and
4. Require the GSO Designate to:
a. Prepare the SSMIs weekly, supported with RIS for supplies and
materials issued and the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV as
supported by SSMIs and RIS submitted by the GSO to record
issuance of supplies and materials; and
b. Prepare and maintain stock cards for each inventory item, even in the
absence of a stock room, to track the movement of the inventories,
including both receipts and issuances, hence, ensuring that no item is
overlooked and that the balance on hand can be easily determined at a
given time.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Examine the discrepancies in the abovementioned accounts and make the
required adjusting entries to reflect the accurate balances. Henceforth,
balances of other affected accounts, if any, in the financial statements shall
also be reviewed and corrected;
2. Ensure that the distribution of shares of the Province and the component
Barangays from the RPT collections is accurately calculated and recorded
as provided for under the NGAS Manual for LGUs and R.A. No. 7160; and
3. Prepare the correct and appropriate accounting entries in relation to
RPT/SET collections in the succeeding years to ensure accuracy and fair
presentation of the accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Conduct a detailed review and analysis of the Inter-Agency Payables
accounts, including confirmation and reconciliation with the concerned
government agencies, to come up with the correct unremitted balances of
the subject accounts to ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.
Thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted;
2. Upon reconciliation, remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations
to the concerned government agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties and
surcharges for delay or non-remittance; and
3. Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of account balances and closely
monitor the preparation of the needed schedules for the remittance of the
withheld/deducted amounts and ensure its remittance in full within the
prescribed deadlines.

1.13 We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct the physical count of
all PPEs to establish the existence, validity and accuracy of PPEs and
ensure timely and complete submission of the RPCPPE to the Office of the
Auditor;
b. Municipal Accountant and GSO to prepare their respective reports and
work closely in the reconciliation and updating of their records; and

1.14 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor avail of the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take immediate action to
ensure the complete and accurate recognition of depreciation expenses in
compliance with the guidelines of the International Public Sector Accounting
Standards. Furthermore, consistent monitoring and periodic review should be
implemented to prevent similar issues from occurring in the future.

3.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to obtain copies of the bank statements from the
depository banks; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete BRSs of all
bank accounts maintained by the Municipality and to henceforth, comply
with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the
preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements to the
Office of the Auditor.

4.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Maintain the Subsidiary Ledgers per agency/fund source to support the
General Ledger balances; and
b. Establish whether the amounts contained in the Schedule of Due to NGAs
and Due to LGUs represent the unutilized balances or the entire amount of
fund transfers.

4.10 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer to refund the unutilized amount to the source agency,
once the balances are already established by the Municipal Accountant.

5.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require for the liquidation of all unliquidated cash advances, and
henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting
cash advances to officials and employees with unsettled previous cash
accountabilities pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445;
b. Require all accountable officers and concerned employees to immediately
refund the excess cash advances granted to them;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify and reconcile the negative
balances included in the schedule of unliquidated cash advances and to
prepare the necessary JEV to properly classify the cash advances
accounts.

6.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to trace the unreconciled balances of the Due from Other Funds
and Due to Other Funds and make necessary adjustments, where appropriate,
to ensure fair presentation of reciprocal and other associated accounts in the
financial statements.

7.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Treasurer to exert efforts in reconciling their records on real property taxes
to establish the actual RPT/SET Receivables accruing to the Municipality in
order to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the
beginning of the year of a duly certified list showing the names of the
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which shall be the
basis of the latter to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables to conform with
Sections 19 and 20, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

8.14 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory count at
least twice a year to establish the actual stock on hand as of the end of a
given period and to determine whether there were any losses or spoilage
in order to fairly present the inventory accounts in the financial
statements. Moreover, submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report of
the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively;
b. Require the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and
maintain the Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Card for each type of
supplies; and
c. Instruct the Supply Officer to strictly implement the use of the Requisition
and Issue Slip (RIS) for all supplies and materials taken from stock, and
to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to support accurate and prompt
recording of transactions by the Accounting Unit.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to immediately draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the book reconciling items identified in the BRS.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the:
Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the inventories and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team (AT) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters; and
MA to reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the:
Disposal Committee to institute measures to properly dispose all unserviceable properties pursuant to the procedures set by COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01 dated January 30, 2024;
GSO - Designate to accomplish and update the IIRUP after disposal of  unserviceable properties and furnish copy to the Disposal Committee and AT; and
MA to drop from the books the disposed unserviceable properties.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the MA to:
Prepare the necessary liquidation reports; and
Coordinate with the source agency and reconcile the LGU’s books with DOH – CHD, Bicol to arrive at a correct balance of the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended the LCE to direct the MT to observe the prescribed receipt and collection process, namely:
the RCOs shall remit all collections promptly and intact to the LO and prepare the corresponding RCD on daily basis;
the LO shall check remittances, verify accountable forms of RCOs, consolidate collections, prepare the RCD and remit collections to the MT; and
the MT shall receive remitted collections, consolidate the same, prepare the RCD, record in the Cashbook-Cash in Treasury, deposit collections in the appropriate bank account per authorized depository bank and record deposit in Cashbook-Cash in Bank.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned accountable officers to explain the occurrence missing AFs. If the AF were issued, determine the total amount collected by producing the triplicate copy of the official receipt or other reliable documents. If the AFs lost apply for relief from accountability to avoid invalid/illegal use thereof. Otherwise, the Accountable Officers will be held accountable for the loss.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT to maintain the Cashbook- Cash in Bank and Cashbook- Cash Advance in addition to the Cashbook- Cash in Local Treasury currently maintained by the MT. We also recommended strict compliance with Section 90 (c) of the LTOM.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the MT to keep adequate permanent record books that contain all information set forth in Section 95 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
MT to forward and transmit the DVs and supporting documents of claimed checks and the RCDs together with the copies of ORs and validated deposit slips to the Municipal Accounting Office on time; and
MA to submit within 10 days after the end of the month all DVs and supporting documents and collections and deposits accounts as required under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160.
We recommended the LCE direct the MA to submit the monthly Trial Balances not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the AT the monthly BRS together with the required attachments, for all bank accounts maintained by the LGU within the prescribed period for verification.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to ensure that DVs for infrastructure projects be sufficiently and adequately attached with the mandatory supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to complete the required documentation of the DVs in compliance with Section 5.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.

We recommended that the LCE, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer (ME),  inform the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the AT.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to prepare the approved APP and PMRs using the format prescribed by GPPB and submit the same to the GPPB in accordance with Section 7.3.5 and 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We commended the LGU’s effort in substantially implementing the programmed projects under the 20% DF during CY 2023. However, we recommended that the LCE instruct the:
MPDC to:
Identify and prioritize the development projects that could be viably implemented during planning of PPAs to maximize its utilization; and
Submit to this Office the Accomplishment Report vis-à-vis 20% DF Plan indicating the status of their implementation; and
ME to fast track the implementation and completion of developmental PPAs for the benefit of the LGU’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to submit a copy of the DILG approved GPB and the corresponding GAR to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

1.11 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct the physical count of all PPEs to establish the existence, validity, and accuracy of PPEs and ensure timely and complete submission of the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor;
Municipal Accountant and GSO to prepare their respective reports and work closely in the reconciliation and updating of their records; and
Municipal Accountant to compute and record depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation on all depreciable assets in the books of accounts.
1.12 	Likewise, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

3.10 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to officially transfer the above Property, Plant and Equipment to the Department of Education, by securing authority from the Sangguniang Bayan by way of a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to execute a Deed of Donation or a Memorandum of Agreement. A copy of the journal entry voucher (JEV) prepared by the Department of Education recording the receipt of properties transferred by the LGU should also be submitted to COA.
3.11 	We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to calculate and consistently provide for the correct depreciation of all LGU properties periodically, to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.

5.12 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that a separate authorization from the local sanggunian is obtained before entering into contracts for PPAs charged to lump sum appropriations described in generic terms.
5.13 	We likewise recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the:
Municipal Development Council to ensure that projects included in the AIP are carefully planned and include specific details, such as project location, in addition to the budget amount;
Head of the Implementing Unit to include PPAs funded by the 20% Development Fund in the PPMP, conduct a review thereof and prepare revisions, at least once every six months and submit the same to the BAC Secretariat; and
Bids and awards committee to ensure that all PPAs subject to procurement are included in the APP.

6.9 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Council, Municipal Budget Officer and Department Heads to evaluate the necessity of the identified priority PPAs, which to date remained unimplemented. Otherwise, reprogram the same together with the savings generated from completed PPAs to the much-needed PPAs in accordance with Section 3.2.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1, and to henceforth, properly plan and execute all procurement activities pertaining thereto, to ensure the timely implementation and completion in accordance with Section 3.2.3 of the same circular, so as to ensure the judicious delivery of services to the constituents.

7.8 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the LDRRMO II and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on a monthly basis, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 12 of RA 10121 and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the timely review of the utilization of the LDRRMF.

8.13 	We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the optimum use of the LDRRMF and facilitate the timely implementation of the targeted PPAs.
8.14 	We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The Municipal Engineer to properly verify the status of the project implementation before requests of contractors for the release and substitution of retention money are allowed; and
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that financial instruments submitted to substitute the retention money are in the form and amount allowed under Section 6, Annex E of RA No. 9184.

9.31 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Return the unexpended balance of ₱48,150.00 allocated for the Local Government Support Fund - Financial Assistance to Local Government Units (LGSF – FALGU) to the National Treasury pursuant to Item 4.10 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 142 dated January 24, 2022;
Strictly comply with documentary requirements by securing MOA/Trust Agreements and approved project proposals to form part of supporting documents depending on the nature of transaction in the implementation and liquidation of fund transfers as provided for in Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
Immediately remit the taxes withheld from the HEA of healthcare workers totaling ₱15,325.00, to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

10.7 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the GAD Focal Point System to:
Ensure the implementation of PPAs that address priority gender issues and meet specific GAD mandates and targets to ensure attainment of the LGU’s GAD objectives; and
Strictly comply with the schedule of GAD planning and budgeting by submitting the GPB to the PPDO for review within the period prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-01.

11.14 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that:
All prior cash advances of an Accountable Officer are liquidated before granting another cash advance, following COA No. 2012-001. Likewise, ensure that cash advance shall be given only for a legally authorized specific purpose; and
The amount of cash advance granted does not exceed the maximum accountability of the Accountable Officer. Otherwise, require the AO to apply for an additional bond if the accountability is increased in compliance with COA Circular 97-002.

12.19 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Formulate and adopt internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services of their respective personnel based on the policies and guidelines under CSC-DBM JC No. 02, s. 2015 and submit the same to DBM and CSC. Likewise, submit the "Report on Overtime Services with Pay" to DBM's Budget and Management Bureau or Regional Office concerned, copy furnished the CSC, upon payment of overtime;
Comply with the requirements provided by CSC-DBM JC No. 02 series of 2015 in determining the hours subject to overtime payment to ensure that employees are accurately compensated, minimize the occurrences of overtime abuse and to avoid disallowance; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for overtime are covered with proper and complete documentation and ascertain that daily time records properly reflect the time of arrival and departure. Additionally, manual entries must be duly supported with necessary documents.

14.7 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Bids and Awards Committee to follow proper procurement methods as provided for in Sections 10 and 48 of RA No. 9184; and
Concerned officials and employees to refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursement through reimbursement basis, and to withhold its corresponding taxes to avoid delinquencies and revenue losses on the part of the government, in adherence to RA No. 10963.

observations.managementAction: The LCE issued an agency Office Memorandum No. 4-A s. 2024 dated March 5, 2024 directing the concerned personnel to perform the audit recommen dations. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) RCOs are already implementing the monthly preparation of RAAFs specifying the quantity of the accountable forms received, issued or cancelled as of the end of month by each RCO; Consolidate the RAAFs and submit the same together with the individual RAAFs to the Audit Team not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month in order to facilitate monthly verification of accountable forms; Stop the practice of sharing booklets of AFs. The transfer of AFs from one RCO to another shall be supported with the proper recording of the transfer of accountability so that accountability to AFs are fixed; and Locate and produce the 177 unaccounted accountable forms. Meanwhile, records show that for CY 2024, no individual RAAF was submitted to the Audit Team (AT) other than the aforementioned submission of AO 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the concerned accountable officers to explain the occurrence missing AFs. If the AF were issued, determine the total amount collected by producing the triplicate copy of the official receipt or other reliable documents. If the AFs were lost, apply for relief from accountability to avoid invalid/illegal use thereof. Otherwise, the Accountable Officers will be held accountable for the loss.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued an agency office memorandum No. 4-B s. 2024 dated March 5, 2024 to the concerned MTO accountable officers requiring them to submit an explanation for the occurrence of missing AFs. The 177 unaccounted accountable forms were already transmitted to the audit team. Some cited AFs were found to be issued. However, the concerned MTO submitted the accountable officers explanation to the AT. On the other hand, some of the cited AFs remained unaccounted. (See Annex A for details) The 177 unaccounted accountable forms were already transmitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MT to maintain the Cashbook-Cash in Bank and Cashbook-Cash Advance in addition to the Cashbook-Cash in Local Treasury currently maintained by the MT. We also recommended strict compliance with Section 90 (c) of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued an agency office memorandum No. 4-C s. 2024 dated March 5, 2024 requiring the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the Cashbook-Cash in Bank and Cashbook-Cash Advance. The Cashbook-Cash in Bank and Cashbook-Cash Advance are currently maintained by the MT.

- the LO shall check remittances, verify accountable forms of RCOs, consolidate collections, prepare the RCD and remit collections to the MT; and

observations.managementAction: No.4-f dated April 15, 2024 directing the Municipal Treasurer to observe the prescribed receipt and collection process in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. The Municipal Treasurer’s office is now implementing the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive directs the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to forward and transmit the DVs and supporting documents of claimed checks and the RCDs together with the copies of ORs and validated deposit slips to the Municipal Accounting Office on time; and
b. Municipal Accountant to submit within 10 days after the end of the month all DVs and supporting documents and collections and deposits accounts as required under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued an agency office memorandum No. 4-G s. 2024 dated April 15, 2024 directing the municipal Treasurer to forward and transmit the DVs and supporting docs on time and Municipal accountant to submit within 10 days after the end of the month all DVs and supporting docs and collections and deposits accounts to the Audit Team. All DVs were transmitted on a timely basis, however, DVs for government remittances like HDMF and GSIS took weeks or over 1 month before the agency could release the official receipt. The MAO was unable to submit the DVs and supporting documents within 10 days after the end of the month. Actual submission is as follows: June – July 26, 2024; July – Aug. 29, 2024; Aug. – Oct. 3, 2024; Sept. – not submitted as of Oct. 30, 2024. Additionally, the MAO was unable to address issue on being under staffed.
observations.reason: MAO was understaffed causing delays in submission. DVs for some government remittances took weeks before release of OR.

Submission of Monthly Trial Balances remained delayed. Actual submission is as follows: June – Aug. 1, 2024; July – Aug. 15, 2024; Aug. and Sept. – not submitted as of Oct. 30, 2024.

observations.managementAction: The LCE issued an agency office memorandum no. 4-I s. 2024 dated April 15, 2024 directing the municipal accounting office to submit the monthly BRS together with the required attachments, for all bank accounts maintained by the LGU within the prescribed period. Submission of Monthly BRS remained delayed. Actual submission is as follows: June – July 26, 2024; July – Aug. 20, 2024; Aug. and Sept. – not submitted as of Oct. 30, 2024.

The LCE issued memo no. 4-O s. 2024 dated April 23, 2024 directing Heads of Offices to render an explanation regarding the absence of APP for purchase of tickets. An explanation by department heads was required during MANCOM meeting and all have committed to comply: A; to include in their PPMP all acquisitions of airline tickets planned for the year; B; to ensure that no procurement will be authorized unless in accordance with an approved APP; C; Instructed all officials and employees to purchase airline tickets directly from commercial airlines and service providers. For verification in the CY 2025 APP.

The Municipal Treasurer to obtain the addresses of payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement; prospectively, closely monitor and send written notices to the payees one month before the checks become stale; ensure that stale checks are reported and attached to the RCI prepared on the period of cancellation; return the original DVs and supporting documents of unclaimed stale checks to the Municipal Accountant; issue a replacement check upon submission/presentation of the stale check in the hands of payees; and coordinate or report to the bank about 8 checks in her name and encashed by the bank but which were not drawn against the depository account of the LGU as per bank statements and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant about the result of the foregoing. JEV was prepared to revert back the transaction. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to revert back the transaction to the appropriate cash and liability accounts upon the return the original DVs and supporting documents of unclaimed stale checks in the MTO.

observations.recommendation:

MPDC to: a. Identify and prioritize the development projects that could be viably implemented during planning of PPAs to maximize its utilization; and b. Submit to this Office the Accomplishment Report vis-à-vis 20% DF Plan indicating the status of their implementation; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry vouchers to record
all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the books
for fair presentation of the balance of Cash in Bank-LCCA-LBP account in the
financial statements;
b. augment the workforce of the Municipal Accounting Office in order to
immediately locate the documents and determine the details pertaining to the
unrecorded disbursements and unidentified reconciling items for proper
adjustment in the Municipality’s books; and
c. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the inventory of
PPE, indicate whether the items counted are under GF or SEF and prepare a
summary per class of PPE and submit thereon to the Audit Team;
b. instruct the OIC-GSO to update Property Cards for all PPE accounts and the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each
category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land to substantiate the
correctness of the general ledger balances for PPE accounts; and
c. require the reconciliation of Accounting and GSO records. Any discrepancies
found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustments should be made
where the error was committed.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the inventory of
Municipality’s Local Road Networks, indicate the required details such as
Road Network ID No. and description of each road component and submit
thereon to the Audit Team;
b. instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to maintain subsidiary
records for roads and road components and Local Road Network Property
Card to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balance for
Infrastructure Assets - Road Networks account, and;
c. require the Accounting and GSO to reconcile their records. Any discrepancies
found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustments should be made
where the error was committed.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
exert extra effort to retrieve the related documents/records and conduct a detailed
review of the accounts and investigate/determine the causes of the unreconciled
amounts; and thereafter, prepare the required adjusting journal entries in the
books of accounts to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the
financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents
relative to purchase of lands to substantiate the disbursements made. Otherwise,
the said transactions shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the MHRMO to coordinate immediately with DBP regarding the status of
the MOA; and
b. formally request from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to pass a resolution for
termination of the MOA if the same is no longer implementable and for the
immediate transfer the idle cash amounting to P10,031,067.33 to the active
bank account of the Municipality.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
with the OIC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net
differences of P1,952,341.15 and P(3,245,782.12) between the recorded
balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their
reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province;
b. instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required
adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in
the Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of
accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the
corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
continue exerting extra efforts in retrieving the JEV or other related documents,
conduct thorough analysis of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the
difference in the reciprocal accounts and thereafter, make the necessary adjusting
entry to correct the records.

Officer to ensure the immediate and proper implementation of the program
funded by the fund transfer from Source Agency; and henceforth, observe the
provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing programs funded by the
Source Agencies so as to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely
manner.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Human Resource Officer (HRMO) to publish the vacancy
and expedite the hiring of the mandatory positions and other prescribed
positions; and
b. fill out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Section 443(a) of RA No. 7160
and in accordance with existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil
Service Commission, for more effective administration of the said offices which
in turn will benefit the Municipality as a whole.

Direct the MA to:

Effect the necessary adjustments relative to the book reconciling items with available supporting documents.

Direct the MA to record in the book of accounts the above stated unrecorded disbursements upon submission of the DVs and its supporting documents by the MT in order to reflect an accurate balance of cash, expense and equity accounts of the LGU in the financial statements. Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account, certified by the Accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity; b. Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim; and c. Other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter-request.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the: a. Municipal official and employees with outstanding cash advances to immediately liquidate/settle their accountabilities, if warranted, impose the withholding of salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to certify each claim for cash advance that the person drawing such has no outstanding previous cash advances prior to processing the claim; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to send Demand Letters to the accountable officers who are resigned or separated from service to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances. Conduct periodic review and monitoring of actual performance so that appropriate measures could be undertaken to achieve targets and further improve collection efficiency; and

Provide targets for accounts without projections based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and other departments involved in generating revenues to intensify further collection of revenues that missed the targets.

invitations are received at least five calendar days before the date of the
procurement stage/activity to enhance the transparency of the process; and

b. instruct the BAC Secretariat through the BAG Chairman to strictly comply with
the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS website of Notice of Award and
Notice to Proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of
goods, infrastructure projects and services as required under Sections 37.1.6
and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to improve transparency in the government.

We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit within the prescribed period all their
accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the
abovementioned regulations, to facilitate timely review/post-audit of the
financial transactions of the Municipality.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
BAC to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in
printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with
the provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to
prepare and submit to the Audit Team the reports containing all the information
required under the said COA Circular to promote transparency and
accountability regarding the PPAs undertaken by the Municipality, and to
facilitate the validation and verification of the same by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to
strictly adhere with the above cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 for
timely and objective evaluation of the same by the Audit Team, so that the
Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted
thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile immediately their records and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Treasury account.  Henceforth, reconcile regularly the GL and the cashbook so that reconciling items could be properly and timely recorded.

We commended Management for submitting the RPCPPE for CY 2019.   We also recommended that the agency keep up the commendable feat of coming up with a reconciled balance of PPE in the financial statements and RPCPPE.

We recommended that the LCE take necessary action on the previous recommendations made.  Also, we still recommended that the LCE require the former Municipal Mayor and the then Acting Municipal Accountant to explain why payment was made despite the deficiencies noted and inconsistencies of supporting documents. Likewise, require the OIC-Municipal Engineer why the Individual Program of Works prepared was not in consonance with the LPRAT Resolution which was the basis for funding.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the necessary adjusting entry to correct the error in recording the particular infrastructure assets.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the necessary adjusting entries to close the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that management prioritize the approval of the CLUP and revisit its CDP and ensure that climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction activities will be integrated in its land use plan.

We commended the agency’s compliance with previous audit recommendation of facilitating the closure of the dumpsite and construction of sanitary landfill.  However, we recommended that the Municipality facilitate the immediate construction of temporary containment building and other required structures to ensure that residual wastes are properly handled and would not contaminate the ground.

We recommended that the LCE direct the HRM Officer to monitor strictly that all payrolls are supported with properly approved DTRs and Applications for Leave.
We also recommended that the Accounting Office ensure that payrolls, and all other accounts for that matter, are supported completely with required documents before submitting these to the office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Accounting Office all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts.  Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to remit fully all contributions withheld on employees within the prescribed period to avoid restriction  in access to services and benefits of personnel.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to release promptly the RPT shares of each barangay within five days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the attachments as required under Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No, 2013-1 and provide sufficient details of the activities undertaken to facilitate validation of GAD programs and projects reported.

We recommended that the LCE, through the created Appraisal and Disposal Committee, facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties and waste materials in conformity with the provisions of Section 379 of RA 7160 and any other pertinent issuances on property appraisal and disposal.

We commended Management for a job well done.  However, we still recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to continue utilizing optimally the 20% EDF to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes envisioned thereto.

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all PPAs funded from the 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.  We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer reconcile their records and correct any deficiency.

We recommended that the LGU improve its revenue collection efficiency by observing the following measures:
The Local Finance Committee:
Conduct a systematic and realistic  forecasting  of  revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Conduct periodic review and monitoring of actual  performance so that appropriate measures could be undertaken  to  achieve  targets and further improve collection efficiency; and
Provide targets for accounts  without  projections  based  on  statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and other departments involved  in  generating  revenues   to   intensify   further collection of revenues that missed the targets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property and prepare and submit the required inventory report thereon. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, to continue exerting efforts to trace back the details of the unsubstantiated balances of the PPE accounts in order to provide the correct depreciation for each type of property of the Municipality; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review of the Construction in Progress account so that the dormant balance can be adjusted accordingly to its correct account.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted an incomplete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property. Management stated that no documents and records pertaining to CY 2013 and previous years were retrieved. Detailed review was not conducted by the OIC-Municipal Accountant due to unavailable records and documents pertaining to dormant accounts.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their bank account regularly, and effect the necessary adjustment to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accuracy and correctness of the reconciling items reflected in the bank reconciliation statements, particularly the unrecorded disbursements and deposits, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with the adjusted balance of the Cash in Bank account; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate with each other with regards to the accumulation of stale outstanding checks which should have been adjusted either as a liability account or a prior year's adjustment provided an evidence should be presented to support the same.
observations.managementAction: Periodic Reconciliation was conducted by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer however they could no longer retrieve the documents and record pertaining to February 2019. The Officer in Charge of the Bank Reconciliation Statement recorded some reconciling items with supporting documents in the books of accounts as of November 30, 2019.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue exerting efforts in retrieving the JEV or other related documents and conduct thorough analysis of the accounts to determine the cause/s of the difference in the reciprocal accounts; and thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the records.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant already conducted an analysis of the affected accounts. However, the difference in the reciprocal accounts was not adjusted accordingly.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and Treasurer to verify the discrepancies and subsequently prepare required adjusting journal entries to present true balances in the financial statements. Likewise strictly enforce collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be valid.
observations.managementAction: The Management already provided additional documents to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the LCE to direct the: a. Municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to immediately liquidate/settle their accountabilities, if warranted, impose withholding of salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to certify each claim for cash advance that the person drawing such has no outstanding previous cash advances prior to processing the claim; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to send Demand Letters to the accountable officers who are resigned or separated from service to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances. If proven futile, Management should hold accountable the official/s who signed their clearance for the payment of the same. Also reiterated that the LCE consider filing a request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advance of accountable officers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Management issued Notice of Unliquidated Cash Advances, however, the withholding of salaries for those who fail or refuse to comply was not implemented. Demand letters were sent to the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances. The OIC-Municipal Accountant already issues certification prior to processing the claims of the accountable officers.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. make a detailed review of the account in coordination with the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to determine the cause of the discrepancy and make the necessary adjusting entry thereof; and b. request from the GSIS an updated statement of accounts of the Municipality so that they could settle immediately any outstanding liability with the System. If proven none, the Municipality should trace and return any outstanding amount withheld to the municipal employees.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant already coordinated with the HRMO, however, necessary adjustment was not yet made for the noted discrepancy.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive address the underlying issue on the manpower constraint in the Accounting Office to comply with the submission of accounts and financial reports to the Audit Team, within the period prescribed by the abovementioned regulations for timely review and post audit of their accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management belatedly submitted on March 27, 2019 the disbursement vouchers and its corresponding journals for the month of December 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary line item in the annual budget at least one percent of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment exclusively for the strengthening and implementation of Programs, Projects and Activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality allocated one percent of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC’s PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat to submit the required documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate the review and verification of the same and the timely preparation of the required reports.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the status report of the projects funded under the LGSF-AM for CY 2018 including the related documents to the Auditor. However, perfected contracts for CY 2019 remained unsubmitted as of year-end.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. instruct the MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP annually, which will be integrated in the AIP of the Municipality; and b. direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to closely coordinate with each other in order to prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team through the LDRRMC and LDC.
observations.managementAction: No LDRRMFIP was prepared for CY 2019. Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was prepared by the OIC-Municipal Accountant but belatedly submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the agency discontinue further payment of registration fees, travelling expenses and per diems of Members of the Sangguniang Bayan while pursuing master’s degree.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive {LCE} require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to submit written explanation why they charged their claims for registration fees, travelling expenses and per diems against public funds despite the fact that pursuing a master’s degree is considered personal and that there is no appropriation for this purpose.
observations.managementAction: SB Members submitted appeal for the disallowance amounting to ₱680,980.00

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Accounting Office all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts,. 

Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the concerned offices to comply with the COA recommendation on submission of accounts and reports on time. The concerned offices exerted efforts to comply with the timely submission and submit the accounts and reports within the following month.
observations.reason: Although the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant exerted efforts to comply with the submission of accounts and reports within the following month, still there are unforeseen and uncontrolled circumstances that causes delay in preparation of reports and submission of accounts and reports.  The payees took time to claim their checks and cash payments, reason why MTO cannot submit some of the paid vouchers to accounting office immediately and the accounting office to prepare the reports on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require  all employees’ loan applications to government and private financial institutions shall be submitted first to the Municipal Accountant who will note in the loan application the present net take home pay of the loan applicant.

We recommended that the LCE disapprove loan application of all employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.00.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall monitor the take home pay of employees and the payroll preparer shall observe strictly that employees shall receive the mandatory ₱5,000.00 net take home pays in compliance with Section 48 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2018.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate/settle promptly her cash advances within five calendar days after the end of the pay period.

We also enjoined the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with and remind the Municipal Treasurer of prompt liquidation of cash advances to facilitate the preparation and submission of reports to source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate/settle the cash advances promptly. The municipal accountant also coordinated and reminded the municipal treasurer from time to time for the prompt liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: The municipal treasurer took effort to liquidate/settle her cash advances within the prescribed period, some are still submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Human Resource Management Officer to monitor strictly that all payrolls are supported with properly approved DTRs and Applications for Leave.

We also recommended that the Accounting Office ensure that payrolls, and all other accounts for that matter, are supported completely with required documents before submitting these to the office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The LGU assured compliance to audit recommendation and strict monitoring of payment of salaries to ensure that all payrolls are supported with approved DTRs and application for leave.
observations.reason: Although attachment of DTRs and Applications for Leave to payrolls were being monitored, still there were payments of salaries not supported with such documents.  There was significant decrease in the number of personnel without the documents in CY 2019 compared with the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to desist from early payment of salary be made in their future transactions in conformity with Section 88 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  Instead, pay salaries either every fifteen days or once every month as long as services have already been rendered.

We commended Management for the effective implementation of GAD PPAs in CY 2018.  We also recommended that the agency through its LCE prioritize full implementation of all GAD PPAs in succeeding years.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately and intact all collections in her custody as of receipt of this AOM and demand for immediate presentation of corresponding deposit slip/s.

Henceforth, she shall deposit within the prescribed period the full amount received or collected by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in compliance with Section 32, Manual on the New Government Accounting System (For Local Government Units) and Section 8 of COA Circular 93-382.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing, furnished the Auditor, why she should not be penalized for the practice of keeping in her custody huge amount of collection.

Henceforth, she shall deposit within the prescribed period the full amount received or collected by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in compliance with Section 32, Manual on the New Government Accounting System (For Local Government Units) and Section 8 of COA Circular 93-382.
observations.managementAction: The LCE designated liquidating officer who is in charge of report of collections and the deposit of remitted statement of collections. The management did not take action on this recommendation.
observations.reason: By designating liquidating officer and strictly monitoring the deposit of collections, the cash in treasury balance was reduced. The Municipal Treasurer was unable to submit her written explanation to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to ensure that in the planning stage of the PPAs funded under 20% EDF, projects to be included should be doable for implementation on that particular budget year and that funds are optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of the programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio economic development.
observations.managementAction: Substantial accomplishments were noted in CY 2019 implementation of 20% EDF posting 75 per cent accomplishment for completed projects utilizing ₱12.47 million.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality prepare a closure and rehabilitation plan, submit the same to the EMB Regional Office for review and approval and cause the before proceeding with the closure of the LGU’s existing CDF.
observations.managementAction: Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan was already approved by EMB-V, and on-going implementation. Requested for technical assistance for establishing alternative technology for final waste disposal. The LGU is not financially capable to construct/establish sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Requested for technical assistance for alternative technology. The LGU allocated fund for the construction of Temporary Residual Containment Area. Also, fund was provided for the acquisition of Bio and Plastic Shredder Machine for alternative technology for final waste disposal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a) Require the HRMO together with the concerned DHs or create a committee that would formulate standard policy guidelines to strengthen internal controls on hiring/rehiring of JOs with the end in view of:

a.1 limiting the number of hired/rehired personnel based on actual manpower need by each office;

a.2 assuring that JOs do not perform functions of existing regular employees and declaring that there will be no duplication of works among JOs assigned in one Office; and

b) Require the JOs to submit Accomplishment Reports detailing actual works done for the month.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO already submitted letter to Vice Mayor reminding about the audit findings on hiring of JOs and the COA recommendation. Job description of all JO’s are clearly stated in the JO’s contracts. All JOs submitted DTRs and Accomplishment reports attached to every payroll. Hiring of JO’s was limited in compliance with the COA recommendation.
observations.reason: Number of JO’s hired for CY 2019 was still high. Duplication of work among JO’s still evident.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all programs/projects/activities funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of CY 2019 70% allocation of the LDRRMF as of 12.31.19 is 75%.
observations.reason: The LGU implemented in 2019 the 70% allocation of LDRRMF of prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the provincial accountant and verify the discrepancies in account balances of Due from LGU Account and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries. A similar Observation No. 6 is shown in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The municipal accountant already coordinated with the provincial accountant and found out that provincial accounting records of Due from LGUs included assistance to officials, barangays and others of which the LGU was not aware. The adjustment should be from the Provincial Government books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the above mentioned disbursement vouchers complete with supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.  Subsequently, the latter submit the subject DVs to the Auditor for post audit.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise diligence in the safekeeping of disbursement vouchers in her custody and observe strictly the prescribed period of submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the municipal treasurer to locate and submit the disbursement vouchers, and to be diligent in the safekeeping of DVs in her custody and to observe strictly the prescribed period of submission to the Municipal Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The municipal treasurer exerted effort to locate and transmit the disbursement vouchers, although not all was transmitted yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and

Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanguniang Bayan for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer coordinated with the municipal assessor in updating the data to generate more collections and submitted the certified list of delinquencies to the Sanguniang Bayan to assist in the enforcement of collection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the JEV cancelling the 33 stale checks amounting to ₱100,025 and adjust the books based on the following proposed adjusting journal entry:

Cash in Bank – LCCA     ₱100,025
Accounts Payable    ₱100,025

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer monitor the status of encashment and issuance of outstanding checks and declare the checks as stale after the lapse of three months from issuance thereof in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 03-2017.  Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary JEV and adjust the books to recognize the cancellation of stale checks.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer and municipal accountant exerted effort to monitor the status of encashment and issuance of outstanding check to declare the checks stale after the lapse of three months. The LCE directed the municipal treasurer and municipal accountant to implement strictly the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The municipal treasurer and municipal accountant exerted effort to monitor the status of encashment and issuance of outstanding check to declare the checks stale after the lapse of three months, but monitoring was restricted by receipt of bank statement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the designated GSO personnel to conduct physical inventory of its properties and render a report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.  Exercise of due diligence in the conduct of physical count and preparation of inventory report is required from the GSO personnel, otherwise, suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 may be imposed until such time that they have fully complied with the requirements of the Commission.

We also recommended that the LCE ensure the proper monitoring of the progress of inventory taking.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to request approval from the source agency to use the excess funds for some other programs/projects, otherwise require reversion of the same to the National Treasury as mandated by the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Accountant to comply with audit recommendations. The Municipal Accountant is computing final balances of the account before requesting from source agencies.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is computing final balances of the account before requesting from source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the Accounts Payable and revert the balances which had been outstanding for two years or more to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund I conformity with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to further intensify revenue collection efforts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and staff exerted efforts in tax awareness and collection campaign up to regular barangay visitation for campaign and collection.  But, RPT is provincial imposition. Poor collection efficiency is also due to occurrence of calamities.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer and staff exerted efforts in tax awareness and collection campaign up to regular barangay visitation for campaign and collection.  But, RPT is provincial imposition. Poor collection efficiency is also due to occurrence of calamities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that appropriation for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) out of the one (1) percent of IRA be provided and likewise be utilized for the prompt implementation of the programs and projects in order to optimally achieve the desirable impact of addressing the development and welfare of children and youth as prescribed under DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LGU provided fund for the LCPC but less than 1% of IRA for CY 2019 because the LGU has only limited fund to support all the priority PPAs for the year. The LGU prioritize the provision of funds for the LCPC. For CY 2020, the LGU allocated at least 1% of IRA for the PPAs for the LCPC.
observations.reason: The LGU provided fund for the LCPC but less than 1% of IRA for CY 2019 because the LGU has only limited fund to support all the priority PPAs for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board with the Municipal Health Officer, SWM Coordinator and other concerned agencies provide necessary training for SWM staff to ensure that solid wastes are properly handled and the risk of hazards to the SWM staff and the public are avoided or minimized.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is waiting for the EMB-V response on the request for technical assistance for alternative technology. The LGU allocated fund for the construction of Temporary Residual Containment Area. Also, fund was provided for the acquisition of Bio and Plastic Shredder Machine for alternative technology for final waste disposal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately locate and submit the above mentioned disbursement vouchers complete with supporting documents.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise diligence in the safekeeping of disbursement vouchers in their custody and strictly observe the prescribed period of submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the municipal treasurer to locate and submit the disbursement vouchers complete with supporting documents. The municipal treasurer exerted effort to locate and transmit the disbursement vouchers, although not all was transmitted yet. The LCE directed the municipal treasurer to be diligent in the safekeeping of DVs in her custody and to observe strictly the prescribed period of submission to the municipal accounting office.
observations.reason: The municipal treasurer exerted effort to locate and transmit the disbursement vouchers, although not all was transmitted yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2016;

Stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and

Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously served demand letter for settlement of the outstanding CAs whenever the former Mayor was in the Philippines but the accountable officer refused to receive such doubting the validity of the CAs.
observations.reason: Cash advances totaling ₱31,000 granted in CY 1995 to former Municipal Mayor were still unsettled.  These cash advances are the only remaining accounts for prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the General Services Officer  to:

Facilitate the tagging of all assets of the LGU;

Ensure that property numbers in the RPCPPE are the same number in the property tags and ARE; and

Tag PPE showing complete/required information.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the immediate updating of the inventory tags of PPEs.
observations.reason: Inventory tagging is for updating and in process for the 2019 acquired PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the Accountant to prepare and maintain Construction in Progress Ledger Cards (CIPLC) for each on going infrastructure projects;

Require the General Service Officer/the Treasurer/Property Officer whose powers and duties requires him to take custody of and be accountable for all properties, real or personal owned by the agency  to validate  existence of the ongoing infrastructure projects to attest to the correctness of the accounting records; and

Require the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with copy of contract for all projects undertaken by the LGU to aid him in the preparation of Construction in Progress Ledger Card.

We recommended that the LCE require the Local Planning and Development Officer as well as the GFPS Technical Working Group to facilitate the establishment of GAD Database.

We also recommended that Management ensure that GAD programs, plans and policies are gender-responsive.
observations.managementAction: To make sure that all GAD PPAs identified by the LGU is GAD responsive, Management is heeding the COA recommendation to establish GAD database using the CBMS with the assistance of the DILG Regional Office.  The establishment of CBMS has been included as one of the priority PPAs in 2020 GAD of the LGU.
observations.reason: The LGU has an existing GAD database base, albeit computer-aided, which is utilized to ensure that GAD PPAs are identified GAD responsive.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the General Services Officer or the person-in-charge of the trip tickets to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as required under Section 361.g of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I.
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1 per cent of the total budget for Senior Citizens and PWDs.
observations.managementAction: The LGU prioritize the provision of funds for the Senior Citizen and PWDs. For CY 2020, the LGU allocated at least 1 per cent of IRA for the PPAs for the Senior Citizen and PWDs. The LGU has already established Municipal Council for the Elderly and PWDs.
observations.reason: The LGU provided funds for the Senior Citizen and PWDs but less than 1 per cent of IRA for CY 2019 because the LGU has only limited fund to support all the priority PPAs for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate closure of LGU existing controlled dumpsite and convert it into a sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Mayor sent letters to the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Engineer seeking for their explanation but got no reply. The incumbent Mayor bowed to send letters to them requiring their explanation why payment was made. The LGU always ensure that proper authority is secured before the LCE signs contracts.

observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.

We also recommended for the Accounting Office to ensure the completeness of the needed documents for all transactions before submitting the same to the office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The LCE required full enforcement of recommendations immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct concerned Accounting and Treasury personnel to exert extra effort in preparing and submitting on time the above mentioned accounts and financial reports including related supporting documents. observations.managementAction: Efforts are exerted towards updating the submission of reports and accounts.
observations.reason: Submission still delayed due to lack of proper system in meeting reporting requirements.

a.  Appraise the market value of the unserviceable properties and waste materials to be approved by COA; and
Dispose the unserviceable properties and waste materials in accordance to the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in Section 79 of RA 7160 which are either through, a)  public auction; b) sale through negotiation; c) transfer without cost to other offices or department or any government agencies and; d) by destruction.
observations.reason:
Unserviceable properties were still not disposed as of December 31, 2018.
Appraisal not performed.

observations.reason:
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption was not prepared and submitted to this office.

We recommended that the LCE require the former Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant to explain why payment was made despite the deficiencies noted and inconsistencies of supporting documents.
