observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s property and prepare and submit the required inventory report
thereon. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and
property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments
where the error was committed;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, to continue exerting efforts to trace back the details of
the unsubstantiated balances of the PPE accounts in order to provide the correct
depreciation for each type of property of the Municipality; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review of the Construction in
Progress account so that the dormant balance can be adjusted accordingly to its
correct account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
bank account records regularly, and effect the necessary adjustment to avoid
accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in
Bank account in their respective records;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accuracy and
correctness of the reconciling items reflected in the bank reconciliation
statements, particularly the unrecorded disbursements and deposits, and
effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with the adjusted
balance of the Cash in Bank account; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to closely
coordinate with each other with regards to the accumulation of staled
outstanding checks which should have been adjusted either as a liability
account or a prior year’s adjustment provided that an evidence should be
presented to support the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to continue exerting efforts in retrieving the JEV or other related
documents and conduct thorough analysis of the accounts to determine the
cause/s of the difference in the reciprocal accounts; and thereafter, make the
necessary adjusting entry to correct the records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant and Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P2,114,500.13 and
P(5,303,170.74) between the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs
accounts of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts Due to and
Due from LGUs account reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the
said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Likewise, strictly
enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be
valid.

We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) to direct the:
a. Municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to
immediately liquidate/settle their accountabilities, if warranted, impose the
withholding of salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the
regulations;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to certify each claim for cash advance that the
person drawing such has no outstanding previous cash advances prior to
processing the claim; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to send Demand Letters to the accountable officers
who are resigned or separated from service to liquidate immediately their
outstanding cash advances. If proven futile, Management should hold
accountable the official/s who signed their clearance for the payment of the
same. See to it that agency clearance would not be issued to any
official/employee unless all their cash advances have been fully settled.

We also reiterated that the LCE consider filing a request for authority to write-off
the dormant unliquidated cash advance of accountable officers pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if applicable.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. make a detailed review of the account in coordination with the Human
Resource Management Office (HRMO) to determine the cause of the
discrepancy and make the necessary adjusting entry thereof; and
b. request from the GSIS an updated statement of accounts of the Municipality so
that they could settle immediately any outstanding liability with the System. If
proven none, the Municipality should trace and return any outstanding amount
withheld to the municipal employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive address the underlying issue on
the manpower constraint in the Accounting Office to comply with the submission
of accounts and financial reports to the Audit Team, within the period prescribed
by the abovementioned regulations for timely review and post audit of their
accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers for the month of December as well as the financial reports
to facilitate the preparation of the related reports required from the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare a Road
Map of the Municipality to support the Local Road Inventory provided by the
Municipal Engineer;
b. Municipal Engineer to ensure that the cost segregation of road components of the
road projects indicated in the Local Road Inventory is correct by providing
documents or additional information to support the same; and thereafter
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks in the books of
accounts in accordance with the accounting procedures of the abovementioned
Circular.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary line item in the annual
budget at least one percent of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment
exclusively for the strengthening and implementation of Programs, Projects and
Activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the posting requirement, pursuant to the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA No. 9184 to attain the widest
circulation of the Municipality’s procurement/bidding activities.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
require the BAC to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the
GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance
with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Bids and Award Committee
(BAC) to submit the required documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed
period to facilitate the review and verification of the same and the timely
preparation of the required reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the designated GSO personnel to conduct physical inventory of its properties and render a report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.  Exercise of due diligence in the conduct of physical count and preparation of inventory report is required from the GSO personnel, otherwise, suspension of payment of salaries pursuant to Section 122(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 may be imposed until such time that they have fully complied with the requirements of the Commission.
We also recommended that the LCE ensure the proper monitoring of the progress of inventory taking.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately and intact all collections in her custody and demand for immediate presentation of corresponding deposit slip/s.  Henceforth, she shall deposit within the prescribed period the full amount received or collected by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in compliance with Section 32, Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs and Section 8 of COA Circular 93-382.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to explain in writing, furnished the Auditor, why she should not be penalized for the practice of keeping in her custody huge amount of collection.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the above mentioned disbursement vouchers complete with supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts. Subsequently, the latter submit the subject DVs to the Auditor for post audit.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise diligence in the safekeeping of disbursement vouchers in her custody and observe strictly the prescribed period of submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate/settle promptly her cash advances within five calendar days after the end of the pay period.  We also enjoined the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with and remind the Municipal Treasurer of prompt liquidation of cash advances to facilitate the preparation and submission of reports to recipient agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the JEV cancelling the 33 stale checks amounting to ₱100,025 and adjust the books based on the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
Cash in Bank-LCCA			₱100,025.00
Accounts Payable			₱100,025.00

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all programs/projects/activities funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned LGU Officials and employees to submit written explanations why they charged their claims for registration fees, travelling expenses and per diems against public funds despite the fact that pursuing a master’s degree was considered personal in nature and that there was no appropriation for this purpose.
Henceforth, we recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to stop immediately charging all expenses arising from any pursuit of master’s degree and the Municipal Accountant to reject any of the said claims.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to desist from early payment of salary in their future transactions in conformity with Section 88 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  Instead, pay salaries either every fifteen days or once every month as long as services have already been rendered.

We recommended that the LCE require all employees’ loan applications to government and private financial institutions shall be submitted first to the Municipal Accountant who will note in the loan application the present net take home pay of the loan applicant.  We recommended that the LCE disapprove loan application of all employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.  Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall monitor the take home pay of employees and the payroll preparer shall observe strictly that employees shall receive the mandatory ₱5,000 net take home pays in compliance with Section 48 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2018.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Human Resource Management Officer to monitor strictly that all payrolls are supported with properly approved DTRs and Applications for Leave.
We also recommended that the Accounting Office ensure that payrolls, and all other accounts for that matter, are supported completely with required documents before submitting these to the office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare a closure and rehabilitation plan, submit the same to the EMB Regional Office for review and approval before proceeding with the closure of the LGU’s existing CDF.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Accounting Office all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts.  Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to ensure that in the planning stage of the PPAs funded under 20% EDF, projects to be included should be doable for implementation on that particular budget year and that funds are optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of the programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio economic development.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We commended Management for the effective implementation of GAD PPAs in CY 2018.  We also recommended that the agency through its LCE prioritize full implementation of all GAD PPAs in succeeding years.

We recommended that Management:
Require the HRMO together with the concerned DHs or create a committee that would formulate standard policy guidelines to strengthen internal controls on hiring/rehiring of JOs with the end in view of:
a.1	limiting the number of hired/rehired personnel based on actual manpower need by each office;
a.2	assuring that JOs do not perform functions of existing regular employees and	declaring that there will be no duplication of works among JOs assigned in one Office; and
Require the JOs to submit Accomplishment Reports detailing actual works done for the month

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a thorough physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality and come up with complete Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Audit Team. This report should be reconciled with the records maintained by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to establish the existence and accuracy of the PPE account balances presented in the year-end financial statements; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to establish and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively, for all PPE items and undertake the required reconciliation of their records.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted a physical count of its properties in CY 2018 although it covered only the current purchases from August to November 2018. There were confusions on the required procedures in conducting a physical count of property and the preparation of the report thereon.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation no. 1, page 30.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances be directed to immediately liquidate/settle their accountabilities. If warranted, impose the withholding of salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations. OIC-Municipal Accountant to certify each claim for cash advance that the person drawing such has no outstanding previous cash advances prior to processing the claim. OIC-Municipal Accountant to send Demand Letters to the accountable officers who are resigned or separated from service to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances. If proven futile, Management should hold accountable the official/s who signed their clearance for the payment of the same. See to it that agency clearance would not be issued to any official/employee unless all their cash advances have been fully settled. We also recommended that the LCE consider filing a request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advance of accountable officers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if applicable.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant already issues certification prior to processing the claims of accountable officers. Demand letters were already sent to the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances on June 18, 2018.
observations.reason: Not all cash advance claims were supported by the certification of the OIC-Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive address the underlying manpower constraint in the Accounting Office to comply with the submission of accounts and financial reports to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by the abovementioned regulations for timely review and post audit of their accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the disbursement vouchers for the month of December as well as the financial reports to facilitate the preparation of the related reports required from the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management belatedly submitted on March 27, 2019 the disbursement vouchers and its corresponding journals for the month of December, and commented that they were amenable with the remaining audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. OIC-Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system; b. Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and thereafter; c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.
observations.managementAction: A road map of the Municipality has already been prepared by the Municipal Engineering Office. The Management already prepared a Local Road Inventory although the cost segregation of road component per project was not yet fully established.
observations.reason: No supporting documents or relevant information was provided to the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. stop approving disbursements which are not included in the list of allowable expenses chargeable against SEF pursuant to Item 4.0 of the DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and pursuant to the Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA No. 7160; and b. direct the Municipal Budget Officer to replenish the SEF from the General Fund in the amount expended for the unauthorized expenditures. Otherwise, these transactions shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management already stopped approving disbursements that are not included in the allowable expenses. The Management already replenished the unauthorized amount expended from SEF thru Supplemental Budget No. 04, Series 2018 dated November 13, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Gender and Development Focal Person to: a. prepare and submit to the PPDO the GAD Plan and Budget and submit the same within the prescribed period to the DILG Provincial Office for review and endorsement; and b. prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team in accordance with the regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018, duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and Designated Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF provided under RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to ensure that the said fund is utilized solely for approved disaster risk reduction and management (DRRM) related programs, projects, and activities; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to replenish the LDRRMF from the General Fund in the amount expended for the fund in nonrelated disaster risk reduction management activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management already replenished the LDRRMF from the General Fund in compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE compel the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her records with the accounting in order to come up with corrected balances for all funds.  All items uncovered during the reconciliation process should be adjusted to ensure the correctness of the balance of this particular account in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to officers and employees with due and demandable cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to request approval from the source agency to use the excess funds for some other programs/projects, otherwise require reversion of the same to the National Treasury as mandated by the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the Municipal Accountant to prioritize identification of balances which were due for reversion and those which could be used by the LGU for other programs.
observations.reason: Still in the process of reconciling accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary entries to transfer the above mentioned DRRMF balances to the Trust Funds Books. We also recommend that reconciliation between the amount pertaining to prior years’ DRRMF balances be made and the necessary adjusting entries be effected in the books of accounts.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to further intensify revenue collection efforts.
observations.managementAction: The agency conducted tax campaigns per Barangay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Inventory Committee to conduct a thorough physical inventory of all properties of the municipality and come up with a complete Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Audit Team. This report should be reconciled with the records maintained by the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer in order to establish the existence and accuracy of the PPE account balances presented in the year-end financial Statements.

	Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs both the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to establish and maintain subsidiary ledgers and property cards, respectively, for all PPE items and undertake the required reconciliation of their records.

	We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintains subsidiary ledger for Construction in Progress account and to make a thorough review and analysis of the booked transactions in order to come up with correct and updated amount in said account.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary adjustments by performing a thorough reconciliation to trace the discrepancies noted in order to present the correct year-end balance of Cash in Bank account.

	Also, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer make the necessary adjustment for the outstanding stale checks by reverting it back to Accounts Payable. Henceforth, once the check became stale make necessary adjustment as required under Sections 59 and 61 of the New Government Accounting System, Volume I, for LGUs.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exerts extra effort to look for the needed records/documents and information so that the depreciation for the said PPE could be computed and provided.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the OIC-Municipal Accountant should make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires:

The officers/employees with outstanding cash advances for more than the allowable period of time or for which purposes have already been served, to settle them by rendering liquidation reports thereon and refund whatever unused amount still in their hands.

The Municipal Accountant to certify each and every claim for cash advance that the person drawing such has no outstanding previous cash advances prior to processing the claim.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant closely monitors outstanding advances of officials and employees and demand immediate settlement pursuant to above cited relevant regulations.  If warranted, withholding of salaries of those who failed to comply with the requirements must be imposed.

	We recommended further that Management considers filing a request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advance of accountable officers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipality discontinues charging against the Special Education Fund the expenses of teachers in attendance to trainings and seminars, grants for teaching and non teaching personnel and other expenses which are private in nature. Likewise, strict adherence to item 4.0 of the DECS, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01-A on the prioritization of expenses chargeable to SEF is enjoined.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to exercise care in recording the municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the account is at all times correct.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to request from the GSIS an updated statement of accounts of the Municipality so that they could settle immediately any outstanding liability with the System. If proven none, the Municipality may consider reverting back to the General Fund the said amount pursuant to existing rules and regulations on the matter.

	Also, we reiterate our previous recommendation that Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to monitor strict compliance with the prescribed guidelines on remittances of withheld payroll deductions to avoid unnecessary penalty charge and also the interest that would be imposed on delayed remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the concerned official to comply strictly with any of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit and submit the required monitoring reports to COA.

We recommended that the Mayor directs the Accountant to submit of all the cited financial records and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by regulations to facilitate the review and verification of the reports and the timely preparation of the required audit reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:

BAC Secretariat to be compliant in posting procurement/bidding notices as required by RA 9184 to attain the widest circulation of the agency’s procurement/bidding activities;
The Municipal officials concerned to submit all the cited required supporting documents and comply with procedures and documentary requirements as required by COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to endure the completeness of supporting documents; and

BAC Secretariat to explain in writing why in the BAC Resolution No. 0033-2016, RM & S General Merchandise was declared the LCRB and not Deyna Builders despite that RM & General Merchandise did not participate in the bidding of the said project.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Pages 28-30 The LCE facilitate the optimum utilization of the 20% EDF and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to closely monitor the implementation of all programs and projects embodied in its 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: Management provide the oversight agencies copies of the approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget and the prescribed Accomplishment Undertaking, supporting documents to comply with the requirements of the law and furnish COA copy of the report with stamped received. Likewise, we recommended the Creation and/or Strengthening of the GAD Focal Point System as required by the Magna Carta of Women (MCW) and following the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) Memorandum Circular 2011-01, that all agencies should have an institutionalized and functional GAD Focal Point (GFPS).
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Pages 30-31 The LCE designated LGUs focal person for GAD to be in Charge of submission to oversight agencies. The LCE committed to fully implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Some of the required documents for submission to oversight agencies were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Municipal Treasurer submits to Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; b. the Municipal Accountant strictly complies with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Pages 31-32 The LCE required the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit DVs, RCDs and ORs within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Submission were still delayed despite previous audit observation, nevertheless, efforts were exerted to improve the submission of required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required the timely submission of the monthly and year-end Financial Statements and other reports/schedules for the General Fund Proper and other funds in accordance with the regulations. Accordingly, Management effect appropriate remedial measures to ensure that year-end financial statements and required reports are submitted promptly; and capacitate agency personnel through attendance to trainings and seminars to ensure an efficient and fast work output.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Pages 32-34 The Municipal Accountant was required by the LCE to observe that submission of report was within the period required.
observations.reason: Volume of transaction of the Municipal Accountant hinders timely submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC, Municipal Treasurer should strictly comply with the above cited provision in the handling of collections. Likewise, avoid- accumulation of cash on hand to prevent loss and misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Page 17-18 The LCE reminded the Municipal Treasurer on her obligation to strictly comply to the requirements of depositing handling-collections.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer is not observant of the requirements of depositing collections intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee and monitored by the Local Chief Executive. b. Thereafter, the Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office should reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts. c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Pages 19-21 The LCE created an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon.
observations.reason: The committee is still in the process of preparing the inventory and render Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the agency GAD Focal Point to strictly comply with joint Circular No. 2004-1 on the submission of GAD Plan to NCRFW for review and should ensure that GAD programs/projects/activities implemented conform to the guidelines set by GAD lead agencies.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Pages 23-24 Audit recommendation already complied with.

observations.recommendation: Management is enjoined to strictly adhere to the regulations relative to the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided in COA Circulars 96-004 and 97-002 to avoid unliquidated balance at year end. Further, we recommended that the concerned Officials and employees be required to settle their cash advances travel within the prescribed period under Section 3.1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013 Pages 20-21 Cash advances were not granted to those with previously unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: Some of the unliquidated cash advances were not yet settled to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit reports on time and strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 to allow timely audit of transactions/accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013 Pages 27-28 Efforts were being exerted to be able to comply with the recommendation of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Lack of proper coordination between the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer is the reason.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Government of Baras should hire job order or contractual personnel based on the demand and necessity of service in consonance with the provision of Section 3.2 of COA Circular 85-55A, Sections 209 and 211 (b) of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 The agency vowed to hire Job Order/contractual personnel based on the demand and necessity of service.
